sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 9.54 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.9)

statement.latex: If$G=HK$is a soluble group and$H$,$K$are minimax groups, is it true that$G$is a minimax group? { D.\,J.\,S.\,Robinson} Yes, it is true (J.\,S.\,Wilson, J.~Pure Appl. Algebra, { 53}, no.\,3 (1988), 297--318; Ya.\,P.\,Sysak, Radical modules over groups of finite rank, Inst. of Math. Acad. Sci. Ukrain. SSR, Kiev, 1989 (Russian)).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
